---
title: "Component B"
---

> **Rename this page** to reflect the actual component (e.g., "Classifier Head", "Decoder", "Loss Module").

## What — Model / Process

- **Model type**: [e.g., linear classification head / regression head / structured output decoder]
- **Process**: maps latent embedding from Component A → task-specific prediction + training signal
- **Input contract**: tensor `h` of shape `[B × H]`, produced by Component A
- **Output contract (train)**: scalar loss; **output contract (infer)**: `{"pred": [B], "prob": [B × C], "meta": {...}}`
- **Key property**: Component B is the only module that touches the task labels `y`; Component A is label-agnostic

## Why — Selection Rationale

- **Primary reason**: [e.g., a single linear layer minimises overfitting when dataset is small; embedding quality from A captures non-linearity]
- **Why not a deeper head**: adding layers duplicates capacity already in Component A, increasing overfitting risk
- **Why not alternative A** *(e.g., SVM head)*: requires separate training step; breaks end-to-end gradient flow
- **Why not alternative B** *(e.g., GP head)*: inference cost scales with training set size; unsuitable for large datasets
- **Necessary condition**: Component A must produce linearly separable embeddings — validate with t-SNE analyzer
- **When to reconsider**: if t-SNE shows non-linearly separable clusters, add one hidden layer to the head

```{dot}
//| label: fig-comp-b-overview
//| fig-cap: "Component B: forward pass with train and infer output paths"
//| fig-width: 6.5
//| fig-height: 3.5
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=TB
  graph [ranksep=0.5, nodesep=0.5]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.6, height=0.42]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph row1 {
    rank=same
    H [label="h  [B × H]", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    Y [label="y  [B]",     fillcolor="#375a7f"]
  }
  subgraph row2 {
    rank=same
    Lin [label="Linear  W, b"]
  }
  subgraph row3 {
    rank=same
    Logit [label="Logits  [B × C]"]
    Sfmx  [label="Softmax  [B × C]"]
  }
  subgraph row4 {
    rank=same
    Loss [label="CE Loss (train)", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
    Out  [label="pred / prob (infer)", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
  }

  H     -> Lin
  Lin   -> Logit
  Logit -> Loss  Y -> Loss
  Logit -> Sfmx  -> Out
}
```

<details>
<summary><strong>How — Formulas and Internal Mechanism</strong></summary>

**Linear projection** (no hidden layer):

$$\text{logits} = h W^\top + b, \quad W \in \mathbb{R}^{C \times H},\ b \in \mathbb{R}^{C}$$

**Training loss** (cross-entropy with optional label smoothing $\epsilon$):

$$\mathcal{L} = -\frac{1}{B} \sum_{i=1}^{B}
  \sum_{c=1}^{C} \tilde{y}_{ic} \log \hat{p}_{ic}, \qquad
  \tilde{y}_{ic} = (1-\epsilon)\,\mathbf{1}[y_i = c] + \frac{\epsilon}{C}$$

where $\hat{p}_{ic} = \text{softmax}(\text{logits}_i)_c$.

**Inference output contract** (`predict_step`):

```python
return {
    "pred": logits.argmax(dim=-1),       # [B]  — integer class index
    "prob": logits.softmax(dim=-1),      # [B × C] — calibrated probabilities
    "meta": {"ids": batch["id"], ...},   # pass-through metadata
}
```

**Class-imbalance handling** (optional): pass `loss_weight` tensor of shape `[C]` to
`nn.CrossEntropyLoss(weight=...)`. Weights are normalised to sum to 1.

**Label smoothing** ($\epsilon > 0$): prevents over-confident predictions; useful when
training data is noisy or when calibration matters downstream.

**Initialisation**: Xavier uniform for $W$; zeros for $b$.

**Config keys**: `method.output_dim` ($C$, auto-set from dataset), `method.label_smoothing`
($\epsilon$, default 0.0), `method.loss_weight` (optional, default None).

| Key | Default | Sensible range | Primary effect |
|-----|---------|---------------|----------------|
| `method.output_dim` | auto | n_classes | output space size |
| `method.label_smoothing` | 0.0 | 0.0 – 0.2 | prediction confidence regularisation |
| `method.loss_weight` | None | per-class floats | class-imbalance correction |

```bash
# Override example
python -m src.main method.label_smoothing=0.1
```

</details>
